rachel_yao.name: Rachel Yao
rachel_yao.summary:
Rachel Yao is a proven champion with 7 tournament titles to go along with 8 Finals and 7 semifinal appearances across 23 tournaments. That kind of consistency at the top doesn't happen by accident — it's the product of relentless preparation and fierce competitive instinct.

A true triple-threat, Rachel competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 4 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 1 mixed doubles title. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: 1 ORC title (across 1 tournament) • U.S. Selection Event top-4 finisher.

Rachel has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 14 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Ethan Qiu — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best result: #2)
  • Justin Dong — a trusted XD partner (7 events, best result: #2)
  • Melvin Cui — 6 events, 1 title together in U13/XD

The 2025-2026 season has been a step up, with 2 titles across 5 tournaments — an improvement that shows Rachel is still ascending. The best may yet be ahead.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Rachel has competed across U11, U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Rachel has competed across 5 different regions of the country (Midwest, NE, NW, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 8 seeded entries across the career, Rachel consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 7 titles to show for it, Rachel is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

Having moved up to U15, Regina is navigating the challenge of tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment period is natural — and with the pedigree Regina brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Regina has competed across U11, U13 and U15, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

Regina has competed across 6 different regions of the country (Midwest, NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 5 seeded entries across the career, Regina consistently earns the respect of tournament committees — a testament to the results put up on the court.

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title to show for it, Regina is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

ryan_ma.summary:
Ryan Ma is a U.S. Junior National Champion — a distinction that places Ryan among the finest junior players in the country. With 15 career tournament victories across 9 tournaments and 25 total entries, this is a player who consistently rises to the biggest moments.

A true triple-threat, Ryan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting 5 singles titles, 2 doubles titles, 3 mixed doubles titles. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Signature achievements: National Champion in BS U19 • 8 ORC titles (across 4 tournaments) • U.S. Selection Event top-1 finisher.

Ryan has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 6 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Veronica Yang — 7 events together in U19/XD, producing an outstanding 5 titles
  • Kyle Wang — 4 events, 1 title together in BD/U19
  • Daniel Zhou — 2 events, 1 title together in BD

With 4 seasons of competitive experience and 15 titles to show for it, Ryan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

ryan_shih.name: Ryan Shih
ryan_shih.summary:
Ryan Shih broke through with a tournament title — a moment that separates contenders from champions. Across 44 entries in 18 tournaments, Ryan has shown the kind of competitive fire that produces breakthroughs, and there are surely more titles ahead.

A true triple-threat, Ryan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a singles best of #2, 1 doubles title, a mixed doubles best of #2. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Ryan has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 15 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Gagan Polineni — 9 events, 1 title together in BD

Having moved up to U17, Ryan is navigating the challenge of tougher competition at the higher age group. The adjustment period is natural — and with the pedigree Ryan brings, a return to the top is a matter of when, not if.

A veteran of the junior circuit, Ryan has competed across U13, U15 and U17, accumulating invaluable experience at every age group. That depth of competitive history is an asset that younger opponents simply cannot replicate.

With tournaments spanning Midwest, NE, South, Ryan isn't afraid to compete outside the home region — and that exposure to different playing styles only sharpens the game.

With 5 seasons of competitive experience and 1 title to show for it, Ryan is a battle-hardened competitor whose journey is a testament to what dedication, talent, and love for the game can produce.

ryan_tiong.summary:
Ryan Tiong has shown serious competitive teeth, reaching the semifinals 1 time among 21 entries across 10 tournaments. Each deep run is a signal that this player belongs among the contenders.

A true triple-threat, Ryan competes across singles, doubles, and mixed doubles — boasting a doubles best of #4. The ability to excel in all three disciplines reveals a player with both the individual brilliance to dominate rallies and the court sense to thrive with a partner.

Ryan has demonstrated remarkable doubles adaptability, partnering with 6 different players across the career. Key partnerships:
  • Pranav Rao — a trusted BD partner (6 events, best result: #4)

Ryan has competed at both U17 and U19, showing the adaptability needed to thrive as the competition grows fiercer with each age group.

Ryan has competed across 5 different regions of the country (NE, NW, NorCal, SoCal, South), demonstrating a willingness to travel and test the game against diverse styles of play from coast to coast.

With 3 seasons of competitive experience under the belt, Ryan brings the kind of tournament-tested composure that only comes from years of stepping onto the court and competing against the best.
